ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Oct-02-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number 263636 (Conservatorship of Lisa Katrina Muschi).

Appearances: Richard Wise for Lisa Muschi (Conservatee). Willis Silverthorne for Berta Quesada (Conservator). Norman MacLeod for Pasqua Muschi (Trust Matter).

Mr. MacLeod stated that discussions had taken place, that the property management was still an issue, and that he had submitted an order for the accounting to be accepted. Mr. Silverthorne stated that Lewis Muschi and his son were not cooperating, and he should be removed as property manager. Mr. Wise informed the Court that the order was acceptable as to form except as to the fees. Mr. MacLeod requested that the Court consider his fees. Mr. Wise informed the Court that his client was receiving funds from the trust, that she was in financial hardship/unemployed, that she had a medical condition, and that the amount of money she was receiving needed to be increased. Mr. MacLeod stated that Pasqua Muschi wished to resign, that her daughters were willing to take over, and Mr. Lewis was willing to step down as property manager. The issue regarding the Conservator's fees was addressed. Both matters were taken under submission. The matter was continued to January 7, 2009 for status on the property management issue.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: J. Alameda./R. Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy W. Yee.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #2, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jun-25-2008 Continued To Aug-20-08 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case #263636 Conservatorship of Lisa Katrina Muschi.

Appearances: for Conservatorship of Muschi: Richard Wise for the Conservatee. Willis Silverthorne for and with Berta Quesada (Conservator).

Appearances: for the Trust matter: Norman Macleod for the Petitioner.

Mr. Macleod stated that there were objections to the accounting filed in April and that they needed more time to sort things out and settlement the matter. Mr. Wise agreed that more time was needed. In addtion, he stated that one of the Co-Owners of the property had died and that there were some serious issues as to the management of the trust property. Mr. Silverthorne stated that he concurred and that he was unable to find the receipt for $3,450.00. The matters were continued to August 20, 2008.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Aguino.
